spec.description: Reusable Grafana 13+ dashboard for native Android and iOS apps instrumented with OpenTelemetry and sending OTLP logs/traces to Grafana Cloud.

options.content:
📱
Android & iOS OTel App Observability
Observability for mobile apps instrumented with OpenTelemetry Android and OpenTelemetry Swift

spec.description: Traces from the QuickPizza Android app. Filtered by the selected Session ID — shows all traces when no Session ID is set.
spec.title: Traces — $session_id

spec.expr: {service_name=~"$service_name"} | session_id=~"${session_id}" | event_name =~ ".+" | event_name != "exception" | event_name != "device.anr" | line_format "[{{.event_name}}]{{if .nav_destination}} {{.nav_previous_destination}} → {{.nav_destination}} ({{.nav_kind}}){{else if .app_screen_name}} → {{.app_screen_name}}{{end}}{{if .app_jank_frame_count}} frames:{{.app_jank_frame_count}} period:{{.app_jank_period}}ms{{end}}"

spec.description: Non-crash errors and caught exceptions — app continued running after these.
spec.title: Errors & Handled Exceptions — $session_id

spec.expr: {service_name=~"$service_name"} | session_id=~"${session_id}" | event_name="device.crash" | line_format "[{{.exception_type}}]{{if .exception_message}} {{.exception_message}}{{end}} | screen:{{.screen_name}} | thread:{{.thread_name}} | battery:{{.battery_percent}}% heap:{{.heap_free}}b net:{{.network_connection_type}}"

spec.description: Uncaught exceptions that caused the app to terminate (event_name=device.crash). Always on the main thread.
spec.title: Crashes — $session_id

spec.expr: {service_name=~"$service_name"} | event_name="session.start" | os_name="iOS" | session_id=~".+" | label_format device_model_name="{{or .device_model_name .device_model_identifier}}", device_manufacturer="{{if .device_manufacturer}}{{.device_manufacturer}}{{else}}Apple{{end}}"

spec.description: Click a session ID to load it in the timeline below.
spec.title: Sessions — click to select

spec.expr: {service_name=~"$service_name"} | event_name="session.start" | os_name="iOS" | session_id="$session_id" | label_format device_model_name="{{or .device_model_name .device_model_identifier}}", device_manufacturer="{{if .device_manufacturer}}{{.device_manufacturer}}{{else}}Apple{{end}}"

spec.description: Metadata for the selected session.
spec.title: Session Info — $session_id

spec.expr: {service_name=~"$service_name"} | session_id="$session_id" | line_format "{{if or .event_name .name}}[{{or .event_name .name}}]{{if .nav_destination}} {{.nav_previous_destination}} → {{.nav_destination}} ({{.nav_kind}}){{else if .app_screen_name}} → {{.app_screen_name}}{{end}}{{if .exception_message}} {{.exception_type}}: {{.exception_message}}{{end}}{{if .app_jank_frame_count}} frames:{{.app_jank_frame_count}} period:{{.app_jank_period}}ms{{end}}{{if .metrickit_diagnostic_crash_exception_type}} signal:{{.metrickit_diagnostic_crash_exception_signal}} mach:{{.metrickit_diagnostic_crash_exception_mach_exception_type}} reason:{{.metrickit_diagnostic_crash_exception_code}}{{end}}{{if .metrickit_diagnostic_hang_hang_duration}} hang:{{.metrickit_diagnostic_hang_hang_duration}}{{end}}{{if .metrickit_diagnostic_cpu_exception_total_cpu_time}} cpu:{{.metrickit_diagnostic_cpu_exception_total_cpu_time}} sampled:{{.metrickit_diagnostic_cpu_exception_total_sampled_time}}{{end}}{{if .metrickit_diagnostic_disk_write_exception_total_writes_caused}} writes:{{.metrickit_diagnostic_disk_write_exception_total_writes_caused}}{{end}}{{if .metrickit_diagnostic_app_launch_launch_duration}} launch:{{.metrickit_diagnostic_app_launch_launch_duration}}{{end}}{{else}}{{__line__}}{{end}}"

spec.description: All log events for the selected session in chronological order. Shows the user's navigation path and what happened.
spec.title: Session Timeline — $session_id

spec.description: App Not Responding events (event_name=device.anr). Main thread blocked >5s.
spec.title: ANRs — $session_id

spec.description: Number of session starts per time bucket — reflects app activity and user volume across iOS and Android
spec.title: Sessions over time

spec.description:
Counts all log events where event_name matches:
- **exception** — handled or unhandled exceptions
- **device.crash** — application crashes
- **device.anr** — Application Not Responding (ANR) events
spec.title: Errors over time

spec.description: Count of unique session IDs — each represents a distinct app session
spec.title: App Sessions

spec.description: Unique devices seen in the selected time range, identified by app_installation_id (Android) or device_id (iOS)
spec.title: Active Devices

spec.description: Count of handled exceptions (non-fatal) — event_name=exception with detected_level=error. App continued running after these.
spec.title: Errors

spec.description: Counts Android crash events reported as event_name="device.crash". iOS OpenTelemetry crash diagnostics are delivered through MetricKit and are not currently included until their event shape is verified.
spec.title: Crashes

spec.description: App Not Responding events — main thread blocked >5s (event_name=device.anr). Android-only. Severe UX failure: app freezes.
spec.title: ANRs

spec.description: Percentage of sessions without Android event_name="device.crash" events. iOS OpenTelemetry crash diagnostics are delivered through MetricKit and are not currently included, so this panel should not be used as an iOS crash-free SLO yet.
spec.title: Crash-Free Sessions

spec.expr: count by (device_model_identifier)(sum by (unified_id, device_model_identifier)(count_over_time({service_name=~"$service_name"} | label_format unified_id="{{or .app_installation_id .device_id}}" | unified_id=~".+" [$__range])))

spec.description: Unique device count per device model identifier
spec.title: 📱 Devices by Model

spec.expr: count by (os_name, os_version_display)(sum by (unified_id, os_name, os_version_display)(count_over_time({service_name=~"$service_name"} | label_format unified_id="{{or .app_installation_id .device_id}}", os_version_display="{{.os_version}}{{if .android_os_api_level}} ({{.android_os_api_level}}){{end}}" | unified_id=~".+" [$__range])))

spec.description: Unique device count per OS and version. API Level shown for Android rows.
spec.title: 📱 Devices by OS Version

spec.expr: count by (os_name, service_version)(sum by (unified_id, os_name, service_version)(count_over_time({service_name=~"$service_name"} | label_format unified_id="{{or .app_installation_id .device_id}}" | unified_id=~".+" [$__range])))

spec.description: Unique device count per app version
spec.title: 📱 App Versions

spec.description: Breakdown of Android crash events reported as event_name="device.crash", by exception type. iOS MetricKit crash diagnostics are not currently included.
spec.title: Crashes by Type

spec.description: Breakdown of non-crash errors and caught exceptions by type. Does not include crashes — those are in Crashes by Type.
spec.title: Errors & Handled Exceptions by Type

options.content:
# 📱 Android & iOS Mobile Observability — OpenTelemetry RUM  

Real User Monitoring for Android and iOS apps instrumented with the [OpenTelemetry Android SDK](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-android) and [OpenTelemetry Swift](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-swift).  

---  

## 🗂️ Tabs  

**Overview** — Session volume, errors, crashes, ANRs, device breakdown. Daily health check. ANRs are Android-only and always 0 for iOS.  

**Session Explorer** — Lists all sessions. Click a row to set the Session ID filter and drill into a single user session across all other tabs.  

**Traces / Logs / Events / Errors / Crashes / ANRs** — Per-signal views, filtered to the selected session when a Session ID is set.  

---  

## ⚙️ Setup  

**Mobile service names** — This dashboard filters on the OpenTelemetry resource attribute `service.name`.

Edit the `Mobile service names` dashboard variable and add the `service.name` values for your Android and iOS apps, for example `my-app-android, my-app-ios`. You can also type a service name directly into the dropdown because custom values are enabled.

This list is intentionally configured manually. Grafana Cloud stacks often contain backend services in the same Loki/Tempo data sources, so an automatic `service_name` dropdown would include unrelated services.  

**Session ID** — Set automatically when clicking a session row. Paste a known ID to jump directly to a session.  

**OTLP endpoint** — Both SDKs send data via OTLP. Configure `OTLP_ENDPOINT` and `OTLP_AUTH_HEADER` in your app. See [Send data via OTLP](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/send-data/otlp/send-data-otlp/).  

---  

## 📚 Further Reading  

| Topic | Link |  
|---|---|  
| OpenTelemetry Android SDK | [open-telemetry/opentelemetry-android](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-android) |  
| OpenTelemetry Swift SDK | [open-telemetry/opentelemetry-swift](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-swift) |  
| OTel concepts | [opentelemetry.io/docs](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/) |

spec.description: Last 10 errors, crashes, and ANRs across selected services. Click a row to expand details.
spec.title: 🔴 Most Recent Errors
Mobile OTel RUM Dashboard
